Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.

## Working a panopt todo

When the user asks you to work on a todo (`do #N`, `start #N`, `work on #N`, etc.), call `mcp__panopt__todo_start` as your first MCP action against that todo *instead of* `todo_get`. It returns the same full detail, flips status to `in_progress`, and claims the `todo:<N>` advisory lock so other agents and the sidebar see the project state correctly. When you finish, call `todo_complete`. If `todo_start` returns `{started: false, held_by: <name>}`, stop and tell the user — another agent already owns it.

input.new_string:
## Working a panopt resource by `#N`

When the user refers to a resource as `#N` (a pound sign followed by a number), the id is shared across todos, notes, agent tools, and processes — don't assume it's a todo. Call `mcp__panopt__id_kind` first to discover the resource's type, then dispatch:

- **Work on / "do" the item** (`do #N`, `start #N`, `work on #N`, etc.) — if it's a todo, call `mcp__panopt__todo_start` as your first action against it *instead of* `todo_get`. It returns the same full detail, flips status to `in_progress`, and claims the `todo:<N>` advisory lock so other agents and the sidebar see the project state correctly. When you finish, call `todo_complete`. If `todo_start` returns `{started: false, held_by: <name>}`, stop and tell the user — another agent already owns it.
- **Plan the item** (`plan #N`) — use `todo_get`, not `todo_start`. Planning is a read; it should not claim the lock or flip status.
- **Read / browse the item** (answer a question about it, render it) — use the kind-appropriate getter from `id_kind`: `todo_get`, `note_get`, `agent_tool_get`, or `process_get`.

# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Workspace layout

Five crates under `crates/`:

- `panopt-core` — transport-agnostic state, persistence (SQLite), filesystem projection. **Must not depend on `rmcp`, `axum`, or `tokio`** — this boundary is the reason core is reusable. Verify with `cargo tree -p panopt-core` if you add a dep.
- `panopt-tool-surface` — the single declaration of the MCP tool surface: `Parameters<T>` types plus a `TOOL_SURFACE` table (name, description, schema-generating function). Both `panoptd` and `panopt`'s `_mcp-proxy` register their tool routes from this table, so the proxy can answer `tools/list` without panoptd being up. **Must not depend on `rmcp`, `axum`, `tokio`, or `panoptd`** — verify with `cargo tree -p panopt-tool-surface`.
- `panoptd` — MCP daemon over Streamable HTTP. Defaults to `127.0.0.1:7600`; bind is configurable via `--host` and every request is gated by a bearer token at `~/.local/share/panopt/token` (`Authorization: Bearer <token>` or `?token=<token>`). Wraps `panopt-core` in a `Mutex`.
- `panopt` — CLI launcher and viewer. Starts `panoptd` on demand, drives Zellij, spawns agent panes.
- `panopt-zellij` — Zellij sidebar plugin. **Excluded from the cargo workspace** because it only builds for `wasm32-wasip1`.

## Build / check / fmt / clippy

`just` recipes (`check`, `clippy`, `fmt`, `test`) sweep both the workspace and the plugin. For narrower work, use cargo directly:

- Workspace: `cargo check -p <crate>`, `cargo clippy -p <crate>`
- Plugin: `cargo check --manifest-path crates/panopt-zellij/Cargo.toml --target wasm32-wasip1` (same pattern for `clippy`, `build`, `fmt`)
- Plugin release artifact: `just plugin-release`

Clippy runs with `-D warnings`. Pre-existing warnings in unrelated code are not yours to fix unless asked.

## Daemon lifecycle

`panopt up` does not restart a running daemon — it connects to it. To force a fresh daemon (e.g., after changing daemon code): `just stop && just up`. `just stop` sends `SIGTERM` twice to clear the clients-connected gate.

Logs: `~/.local/share/panopt/panoptd.log` (tail with `just logs`). Database: `~/.local/share/panopt/panopt.db` (open with `just db`).

## Invariants

- **One per-project id counter (`projects.next_id`) is shared across todos, notes, agent tools, and processes**, not derived from `MAX(id)`. A `#N` reference resolves to exactly one resource. Deleting the highest-numbered item must not free that id for reuse. See `db.rs` schema notes.
- **Schema migrations are forward-only** via `PRAGMA user_version`. Add a new `V<n>` block in `db.rs` and an `if version < n` step — do not rewrite earlier versions.
- **The plugin never closes panes**, only suppresses them. Swap-in-place is the model: a suppressed pane keeps running hidden, the user owns its lifecycle.
- **Filesystem projection is atomic**: write to a temp file, then `rename` over the target. Never write directly to `.panopt/*.md`.
- **One MCP tool surface declaration**: every tool is exactly one `ToolDef` in `panopt_tool_surface::TOOL_SURFACE` plus exactly one `Tool` enum variant + `dispatch_local` arm in `panoptd/src/handler.rs`. The proxy publishes from the shared table without its own list. Adding a tool that fails to wire the dispatch arm is a compile error (the `match Tool` is exhaustive); adding a `Tool` variant without an entry in `TOOL_SURFACE` is caught by `handler::tests::tool_enum_covers_tool_surface`.

## Style

- Doc comments explain *why*, not *what*. Existing code uses long `///` blocks above functions and `//!` at crate top — match that voice.
- Errors: `thiserror` for typed crate errors, `anyhow::Context` (`.context("msg")?`) at call sites.
- Branches/commits are free-form. Topic branches merged into `main` is the recent pattern, but no enforced convention.

## Coordination plane (MCP)

The daemon exposes todos, notes, locks, agent registry, and roster as MCP tools. Each MCP connection is scoped to one project via `?ws=<absolute-path>` on the URL. State mirrors to `.panopt/*.md` on every mutation — those files are read-mostly for humans and the Zellij plugin, not a write surface.

## Hand-launching a first-class agent

Cockpit-spawned panes are first-class automatically (the launcher injects `PANOPT_AGENT`, `PANOPT_NAME`, `PANOPT_TOKEN` into the pane env). For a Claude Code session started outside the cockpit — or on another machine — use `panopt agent-config` so the session lands with a stable id, a friendly display name, and the bearer token in one shot:

```
claude --mcp-config "$(panopt agent-config --name greg-main)"
```

Without this, the session falls back to the rotating `mcp-session-id` HTTP header as its agent key. The key churns on every reconnect, locks held under old keys get reaped by the 30s idle sweep, and other agents see the connection as a series of unreferenceable UUIDs. See `crates/panopt/src/agent_config.rs` for the resolver defaults (id = `$USER-$HOSTNAME`).

## Working a panopt todo

When the user asks you to work on a todo (`do #N`, `start #N`, `work on #N`, etc.), call `mcp__panopt__todo_start` as your first MCP action against that todo *instead of* `todo_get`. It returns the same full detail, flips status to `in_progress`, and claims the `todo:<N>` advisory lock so other agents and the sidebar see the project state correctly. When you finish, call `todo_complete`. If `todo_start` returns `{started: false, held_by: <name>}`, stop and tell the user — another agent already owns it.

Use `todo_get` only when the intent is to *read* a todo (browse, answer a question about it, render it) — not to begin work on it.

## Reference

- `DESIGN.md` — full architecture, decision rationale, data model. Read this before non-trivial changes to the data layer or MCP surface.
- `HANDOFF.md` — current in-flight work and deferred items.

content.text:
I now have the full picture. Before I finalize the plan, one scope decision genuinely changes the implementation — which pane-creation key surfaces to gate.

Here's what I found:

- **Plugin side is already done.** `spawn_blank_pane` (main.rs:2150) refuses with "cannot create panes from the sidebar" when `sidebar_focused`. Its comment even claims `Alt-N` *and* "the rewritten pane-mode keys (`n`/`d`/`r`/`s`)" funnel through this gate.
- **The config side is the gap.** `render_config`/`up.rs` only retargets `Alt n` (`NEW_PANE_BIND_FROM`→`TO`). The stock Zellij `pane {}`-mode split binds are **not** rewritten, so from a focused sidebar pane, `Ctrl-p` then `n`/`d`/`r`/`s` still hits Zellij's native `NewPane` and shreds the 25% sidebar column.

Stock binds that can divide the sidebar (`zellij setup --dump-config`):
- `pane {}` mode: `n` NewPane, `d` NewPane Down, `r` NewPane Right, `s` NewPane stacked
- tmux-compat mode: `"` NewPane Down, `%` NewPane Right
- (NewTab — `c` / tab-mode `n` — creates a tab, doesn't divide the sidebar)

options.label: pane-mode splits only
options.description: Retarget pane{} mode n/d/r/s to pipe panopt:spawn-blank-pane. Covers the primary Ctrl-p split path. Matches the existing spawn_blank_pane comment exactly.

options.label: pane-mode + tmux-mode
options.description: Also retarget tmux-compat mode " and % (NewPane Down/Right). Fuller coverage of every NewPane surface that can split the sidebar.

options.label: all incl. NewTab
options.description: Everything above plus NewTab (pane/tab/tmux mode). Broadest, but NewTab doesn't actually divide the sidebar — it opens a sidebar-less tab.

I now have the full picture. Here's the plan for #114... I mean **#115**.

## Plan: suppress pane creation when a sidebar pane is focused (#115)

The todo has **two distinct facets**. Your repro is the important, subtle one (B); the keybind gap (A) is the broader half the title names.

### Facet B — the repro: Enter-on-agent splits the sidebar (root cause found)

**Mechanism.** Each of the five sidebar panes is a *separate* plugin instance with its own `slot_pane` (main.rs:463). Your steps:

1. Spawn Agent 1 → it's the visible content pane; every instance's `slot_pane` adopts it (main.rs:1262–1268).
2. From the Todos pane, Enter a todo → `ensure_viewer_in_slot` → `spawn_in_slot` does `open_command_pane_in_place_of_pane_id(agent, …)`, which **suppresses** Agent 1 and points the *Todos* instance's `slot_pane` at the new viewer. The **Agents** instance still has `slot_pane == Some(Agent1)`.
3. The reconciliation in `ingest_panes` (main.rs:1252–1256) only nulls `slot_pane` when the pane is **gone**, not when it's **suppressed**. So the Agents instance keeps a stale slot pointing at the hidden Agent 1.
4. Enter on Agent 1 → `route_pane_to_slot(Agent1, true)` (main.rs:2048). `pane_is_visible(Agent1)` is false → falls to `show_in_slot(Agent1, true)`. There, `is_slot = self.slot_pane == Some(Agent1)` evaluates **true** (stale), so it skips the replace/show branch and just calls `focus_pane_with_id` on a *suppressed* pane (main.rs:2104–2113). Zellij resurfaces the hidden pane by **splitting the focused Agents sidebar pane** — the bug.

**Fix (primary).** Enforce the invariant *"`slot_pane` always references a visible content pane, or is `None`."* In `ingest_panes`, drop the slot when it's suppressed, not only when absent:

```rust
// main.rs:1252
if let Some(slot) = self.slot_pane {
    if !self.panes.iter().any(|p| p.id == slot && !p.suppressed) {
        self.slot_pane = None;
    }
}
```

Then the existing re-adopt at 1262–1268 picks the visible viewer, and step 4's `show_in_slot` takes the correct `replace_pane_with_existing_pane(viewer_slot, Agent1, true)` branch — Agent 1 swaps into the content slot, no sidebar split.

**Fix (hardening).** Also guard `show_in_slot`'s detection so a stale pointer can't misfire within a pre-reconcile tick: `let is_slot = self.slot_pane == Some(pane) && self.pane_is_visible(pane);` (main.rs:2104).

**Residual edge to decide.** If *every* content pane is suppressed (`slot_pane` legitimately `None`) and you resurrect an agent while the sidebar is focused, `show_in_slot`'s `None` branch calls `show_pane_with_id` (main.rs:2108), which can still split the focused sidebar. Mitigation: refuse/redirect when `sidebar_focused` and there's no slot, or focus away from the sidebar first. Not hit by your repro (a viewer is visible), so I'd handle it as a follow-up unless you want it in scope now.

### Facet A — keybind gate (the title's literal ask)

The plugin gate already exists (`spawn_blank_pane` refuses when `sidebar_focused`, main.rs:2150) and its comment *claims* pane-mode `n`/`d`/`r`/`s` funnel through it — but `up.rs` only retargets `Alt n`. The stock `pane {}`-mode split binds still hit Zellij's native `NewPane` and shred the sidebar. Fix: add a `retarget_pane_mode_splits` in `up.rs` mirroring `NEW_PANE_BIND_FROM`/`TO`, wired into `render_config` with a warning + tests.

### Tests
- Unit test in main.rs: `ingest_panes` drops a suppressed `slot_pane` and re-adopts the visible viewer (extends the cluster at main.rs:3244+).
- `up.rs`: `retarget_pane_mode_splits` swaps the pipe in / reports when absent (mirrors `retarget_new_pane_binding_swaps_in_the_pipe_action`).
- Manual: replay your exact repro via `just refresh`.

Two open decisions before I implement:

106	/// `MessagePlugin` keybind whose configuration has any of those metadata
107	/// keys can never match a layout plugin whose configuration does not - Zellij
108	/// launches a *second* plugin instance instead. `zellij action pipe` goes
109	/// through the running session's IPC bus and reaches the existing plugin
110	/// without that mismatch. The cost is a brief transient pane while the CLI
111	/// runs; `close_on_exit true` closes it the moment the pipe is sent.

113	/// Why `--plugin-configuration "mode=todos"`: with five plugin instances
114	/// (one per mode), an unfiltered pipe broadcasts to all five. Narrowing to

117	/// the filter ever fails to match.

119	const NEW_PANE_BIND_TO: &str = r#"bind "Alt n" { Run "zellij" "action" "pipe" "--name" "panopt:spawn-blank-pane" "--plugin-configuration" "mode=todos" { close_on_exit true; }; }"#;

#115 implemented and complete. Summary:

**Facet B — your repro (Enter on a swapped-out agent split the sidebar)** · `crates/panopt-zellij/src/main.rs`
- `ingest_panes` now drops `slot_pane` when its pane is **suppressed**, not only when gone — so a sidebar instance whose slot got swapped out re-adopts the visible viewer instead of dangling on a hidden pane.
- `show_in_slot` requires the slot to be visible (`&& self.pane_is_visible(pane)`) before treating it as the live slot, so a stale pointer arriving before the next PaneUpdate can't `focus_pane_with_id` a suppressed pane (the exact thing that split the sidebar).
- Added unit test `ingest_panes_drops_a_suppressed_slot_and_readopts_the_viewer`.

**Facet A — keybind gate** · `crates/panopt/src/up.rs`
- New `PANE_SPLIT_BINDS` + `retarget_pane_mode_splits`: rewrites the stock pane-mode `n`/`d`/`r`/`s` splits to pipe `panopt:spawn-blank-pane` (mode-exit preserved), wired into `render_config` with a partial/absent warning. The existing `spawn_blank_pane` gate then refuses while a sidebar pane is focused.
- Two tests + updated generated-config comment.

**Not verified by me:** the live interactive replay. The fix is build/clippy/test-clean, but reproducing your exact steps needs a running cockpit — and `just refresh` would tear down your current session, so I left that to you.
